sections.label: THINKING
sections.content: Looking at the current image, the green square has moved further right and is now near/inside the right panel, close to the dark gray frame. Let me check the exact positions to understand the movement pattern and whether the player can enter the frame.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Check current positions of key objects
seg = current_frame.segmentation['nodes']

# Green nodes
greens = [n for n in seg if n['color'] == 'N']
print("Green nodes:")
for n in greens:
    print(f"  boundary={n['boundary']} children={n['children']}")

# 1-pixel centers
centers = [n for n in seg if n['pixels'] == 1]
print("1-pixel centers:")
for n in centers:
    print(f"  color={n['color']} boundary={n['boundary']}")

# White 9-pixel squares
whites = [n for n in seg if n['color'] == 'w' and n['pixels'] == 9]
print("White 9-pixel squares:")
for n in whites:
    print(f"  boundary={n['boundary']}")

# Dark gray frames
frames = [n for n in seg if n['color'] == 'c' and n['pixels'] == 16]
print("Dark gray frames:")
for n in frames:
    print(f"  boundary={n['boundary']} children={n['children']}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

- [30,30]-[32,32] - white square between panels

But I only see one dark gray frame in the output. The right panel's frame might have been absorbed or changed. The green square with black center is now at [30,42]-[32,44], which is very close to where the right frame was at [26,44]-[30,48].
Looking at the image, the green square with black center appears to be near/overlapping the dark gray frame in the right panel. 
The goal might be to move the green square into the dark gray frame. The black-centered green square is now very close to the right frame. Let me try moving RIGHT or UP to see if it enters the frame.
